<!-- Generated by the map-cross-entity-references workflow (29 agents, 2026-06-13), verified against consumer sites in source. Master plan for the #428 biblical data-model pass. Companion to building-cascade-conversion.md (referenced as 'plan §' inline). -->

# #428 Biblical Data-Model Pass — Cross-Entity Reference Blueprint

> **♻ RECOVERED + FACT-CHECKED 2026-06-17** (was lost in the `Sources/docs → docs/dev` shuffle; no delete commit).
> Read this as a **verified cross-entity REFERENCE CATALOG, not a current work-plan** — its core verdicts are
> superseded:
> - **The INVERSION model is reversed by the current ruling.** This doc's central premise — *invert* a
>   target-keyed modifier ONTO the conditioner entity (`Building→Bonus.buildingBoosts`, `Building→Tech`, …) — was
>   **withdrawn**. The settled rule is **keep-on-source / deliveryguy** (`modifier-cascade-spec.md` §6 + §6.1): a
>   modifier keyed by entity B **stays on its source** (the building / civic / …), keyed by B, and is *conditioned*
>   via `enabled` / `per` — it does **not** move onto B. So §1's "37 inversions", the §2 inversion map, §3's
>   framing, and the §6 wave plan are **stale as a plan**. (Direct confirmation: modifier-spec §6.1 says
>   `BuildingInfo.{TerrainYieldChanges, ImprovementYieldChanges, …}` are deliveryguy-owned by the BUILDING and
>   authored on it keyed by the target — **not** inverted, the exact opposite of §2 here.)
> - **The plumbing is superseded too.** Wave 0's "scoped-index machinery / reuse the derived-data-repository
>   skeleton / `CvCascadingModifierBundle`" is the #423 approach that the #428/#430 top-down model (the three
>   machines — tally / modifier / enabler) replaced. Don't reuse it.
> - **The §7 owner-decisions are RESOLVED** by §6.1 (improvement / terrain / religion / unit all keep-on-source,
>   deliveryguy-owned; PropertyManipulators stays self-reading; leader biases stay).
>
> **What stays valuable (why it's kept):** the **catalog** — the 245 cross-entity references with consumer-site
> citations (§2 lists), the cycles / ambiguous owners (§4), and §5's "structural refs that STAY" + dead/derived
> drops. That inventory of *which entity references which, verified against source* is useful regardless of the
> invert-vs-keep direction. Mine it for coverage; re-derive any "where does it live" verdict from the
> keep-on-source / deliveryguy rule, **not** from this doc's inversion column.

Scope: every cross-entity reference across the gameplay Infos, with inversion/cascade verdicts scrutinized against source where load-bearing. Verified consumer sites are cited inline.

## 1. Counts

Total references inventoried: **245**.

| Category | Count |
|---|---|
| `effectRelationship` | 119 |
| `structuralReference` | 126 |
| **Inversions needed** (`inversionNeeded=true`) | **37** |
| **Cascade modifiers** (`isCascadeModifier=true`) | **100** |

Cross-tab that matters for sequencing:

| | cascade=true | cascade=false |
|---|---|---|
| **inversion=true** | 35 | 2 |
| **inversion=false** | 65 | 143 |

The two **inversion-needed-but-NOT-cascade** items are the odd pair to watch: `CvPromotionInfo.FreetoUnitCombats` (→ UnitCombat, a unit-template grant) and `CvImprovementInfo.RouteYieldChanges` (→ Route, a static×static plot read). Both invert *home* without becoming scoped channels — they must not be plumbed through the Game→City cascade.

**Verification note:** I spot-checked the heaviest claims against source. All held: Tech*Changes deposit (CvTeam.cpp:5713, 6119 → `changeImprovementYieldChange`), Route TechMovementChange (CvTeam.cpp:6068 → `changeRouteChange`), EveryoneSpecial* (CvTeam.cpp:4530-4537 → `makeSpecial*Valid`), FreetoUnitCombats consumer (CvUnit.cpp:25971), ExtraFreeBonuses asymmetry (CvCity.cpp:8961/9136), and the Bonus-production three-Info fan-out (Building/Unit/Project all expose `getBonusProductionModifier`).

---

## 2. The inversion map (grouped by DESTINATION entity)

This is the work-list. Each line: `fromInfo.field -> destEntity.newShape`.

### → CvTechInfo (the conditioner = player tech state) — **11 inversions, all cascade (Player scope, except where noted)**
Consolidate ALL into one per-target bundle on the tech: `buildingBoosts` for buildings, `specialistBoosts` for specialists, `improvementYieldChanges` for improvements.

- `CvBuildingInfo.TechYieldChanges -> CvTechInfo.buildingBoosts.{BUILDING}.yield`
- `CvBuildingInfo.TechYieldModifiers -> …buildingBoosts.{BUILDING}.yieldModifier`
- `CvBuildingInfo.TechCommerceChanges -> …buildingBoosts.{BUILDING}.commerce`
- `CvBuildingInfo.TechCommerceModifiers -> …buildingBoosts.{BUILDING}.commerceModifier`
- `CvBuildingInfo.TechHappinessChanges -> …buildingBoosts.{BUILDING}.happiness` (plan §43)
- `CvBuildingInfo.TechHealthChanges -> …buildingBoosts.{BUILDING}.health` (plan §43)
- `CvBuildingInfo.TechSpecialistChanges -> …buildingBoosts.{BUILDING}.freeSpecialists.{SPECIALIST}` (hand-rolled int** today)
- `CvSpecialistInfo.TechHappinessTypes -> CvTechInfo.specialistBoosts.{SPECIALIST}.happiness`
- `CvSpecialistInfo.TechHealthTypes -> CvTechInfo.specialistBoosts.{SPECIALIST}.health`
- `CvImprovementInfo.TechYieldChanges -> CvTechInfo.improvementYieldChanges.{IMPROVEMENT}` (**Team scope** — deposited via `changeImprovementYieldChange`, CvTeam.cpp:6119)
- `CvRouteInfo.TechMovementChanges -> CvTechInfo.routeMovementChanges.{ROUTE}` (**Team scope** — `changeRouteChange`, CvTeam.cpp:6068)

### → CvBonusInfo (the conditioner = player/city has the bonus connected) — **14 inversions, all cascade**
The bonus already owns the *mirror* fields (`Health`/`Happiness`/`YieldChanges`, all `inversionNeeded=false`). These land *alongside* them. City scope unless noted.

- `CvBuildingInfo.BonusHealthChanges -> CvBonusInfo.buildingBoosts.{BUILDING}.health`
- `CvBuildingInfo.BonusHappinessChanges -> …{BUILDING}.happiness`
- `CvBuildingInfo.BonusYieldChanges -> …{BUILDING}.yield`
- `CvBuildingInfo.BonusYieldModifiers -> …{BUILDING}.yieldModifier`
- `CvBuildingInfo.BonusCommerceModifiers -> …{BUILDING}.commerceModifier`
- `CvBuildingInfo.BonusCommercePercentChanges -> …{BUILDING}.commercePercent`
- `CvBuildingInfo.VicinityBonusYieldChanges -> …{BUILDING}.vicinityYield` (stricter vicinity gate)
- `CvBuildingInfo.BonusProductionModifiers -> …{BUILDING}.productionModifier`
- `CvBuildingInfo.BonusDefenseChanges -> …{BUILDING}.defense`
- `CvBuildingInfo.BonusAidModifiers -> …{BUILDING}.aidModifier.{PROPERTY}` (Bonus is conditioner, Property is channel index)
- `CvUnitInfo.BonusProductionModifiers -> CvBonusInfo.unitProductionModifier.{UNIT}` (**Player/City scope**, build-cost)
- `CvProjectInfo.BonusProductionModifiers -> CvBonusInfo.projectProductionModifier.{PROJECT}` (**Player/City scope**, build-speed)

(The three Bonus-production fan-outs — Building, Unit, Project — are the same relationship declared from three wrong sides; verified all three Infos expose `getBonusProductionModifier`.)

### → CvImprovementInfo (conditioner = city works the improvement) — **3 inversions, cascade** *(owner-flagged, see §7)*
- `CvBuildingInfo.ImprovementFreeSpecialists -> CvImprovementInfo.buildingBoosts.{BUILDING}.freeSpecialists`
- `CvBuildingInfo.ImprovementYieldChanges -> …{BUILDING}.yield` (City scope; legacy int** + wrapper duplication to reconcile)
- `CvBuildingInfo.GlobalImprovementYieldChanges -> …{BUILDING}.yield` (**Player scope**)

### → CvTerrainInfo (conditioner = city works the terrain) — **1 inversion, cascade** *(owner-flagged)*
- `CvBuildingInfo.TerrainYieldChanges -> CvTerrainInfo.buildingBoosts.{BUILDING}.yield`

### → CvReligionInfo (conditioner = religion present) — **1 inversion, cascade** *(owner-flagged, plan §57 "?")*
- `CvBuildingInfo.ReligionChanges -> CvReligionInfo.buildingBoosts.{BUILDING}.religionSpread`

### → CvUnitInfo (conditioner = building present) — **1 inversion, cascade** *(owner-flagged)*
- `CvBuildingInfo.UnitProductionModifiers -> CvUnitInfo.buildingBoosts.{BUILDING}.productionModifier` (or stay as building cascade keyed by Unit; closer to intrinsic than the rest)

### → CvBuildingInfo (target building B, deposit-at-event) — **5 inversions, cascade**
These are inter-building A→B effects. They invert by **re-homing where the effect lands** (B's effective index), authored on A as a source-deposit channel.
- `CvBuildingInfo.GlobalBuildingExtraCommerces -> B.effectiveCommerce` (Player scope; `buildingExtraCommerce.{B}`)
- `CvBuildingInfo.BuildingHappinessChanges -> B.effectiveHappiness` (City/Player; `buildingHappiness.{B}`)
- `CvBuildingInfo.BuildingProductionModifiers -> B.effectiveBuildCost` (City scope)
- `CvBuildingInfo.GlobalBuildingProductionModifiers -> B.effectiveBuildCost` (**Player scope**)
- `CvBuildingInfo.GlobalBuildingCostModifiers -> B.effectiveCost` (**Player scope**)

### → CvRouteInfo (static×static, **NOT a cascade**) — **1 inversion** *(side-choice, owner-flagged)*
- `CvImprovementInfo.RouteYieldChanges -> CvRouteInfo.improvementYieldChanges.{IMPROVEMENT}` — both sides static; either is defensible. Picked the route as the active grantor. **Do not plumb through the player cascade.**

### → CvUnitCombatInfo (reverse index, **NOT a cascade**) — **1 inversion**
- `CvPromotionInfo.FreetoUnitCombats -> CvUnitCombatInfo.freePromotions[]` — confirmed at CvUnit.cpp:25971 the consumer asks per-combat-class. Reverse-index, unit-template grant, O(1) read. Not scoped.

### → CvTechInfo (restriction, cascade Player scope) — **1 inversion**
- `CvCivilizationInfo.DisableTechs -> CvTechInfo.disabledForCivilizations[]` — `canResearch` (CvPlayer.cpp:8297) asks per-tech; deposit a player-scope "disabled" flag.

### Civic → (conditioner = civic adopted) — **17 inversions, all cascade**
These are special: the right owner **stays CvCivicInfo** (it IS the conditioner), but every one is mis-*shaped* (dense int*/int** that callers sweep) and must become a cascade channel the target reads O(1). I count these as inversions because the inventory marks `inversionNeeded=true` with `rightOwner = CvBuildingInfo/...(target read)` — the *declaration* moves from static-map-the-target-ignores to scoped-deposit-the-target-reads. Destinations by what the deposit lands on:

- → **per-Building index (Player/City):** `BuildingProductionModifiers`, `BuildingHappinessChanges`, `BuildingHealthChanges`, `BuildingCommerceModifiers`, `BuildingCommerceChanges`
- → **per-Feature index (City):** `FeatureHappinessChanges`
- → **per-Improvement index (City/Plot):** `ImprovementYieldChanges`, `ImprovementHappinessChanges`, `ImprovementHealthPercentChanges`
- → **per-Terrain index (Plot/City):** `TerrainYieldChanges`
- → **per-Bonus index (Player/City):** `BonusCommerceModifiers`, `BonusMintedPercents`
- → **per-Specialist index (City):** `SpecialistYieldPercentChanges`, `SpecialistCommercePercentChanges`, `FreeSpecialistCounts`, `SpecialistValids` (enabler)
- → **per-UnitCombat index (Player/City):** `UnitCombatProductionModifiers`
- → **per-Unit index (Player/City):** `UnitProductionModifiers` (kills the readPass3 dance)
- → **per-Hurry / SpecialBuilding flag sets (Player):** `Hurrys` (enabler), `SpecialBuildingNotRequireds` (prereq-waiver enabler)

*(That is 21 Civic effect maps; `CivicAttitudeChanges` is `inversionNeeded=false` — pairwise Civic×Civic, no distinct owner — and `Flavors` is structural AI-metadata. The 17 "inversion-needed" headline count above is **buildings + 1 civ DisableTech**; the Civic maps are tallied separately because the inventory's `inversionNeeded=true` for them means re-shape-into-cascade-on-same-owner, not move-to-another-entity. Treat them as their own wave.)*

---

## 3. Cascade-modifier surface (grouped by scope × kind)

The 100 cascade modifiers form the new scoped-channel surface. The shared channel target across nearly every Info is **Property** (Building/Bonus/Feature/Terrain/Improvement/Unit/UnitCombat/Religion/Corporation/Heritage/Promotion all carry `PropertyManipulators`) — but by explicit ruling (plan §163) **PropertyManipulators stays a self-reading sub-object and is NEVER a cascade channel.** So although ~12 PropertyManipulators carry `isCascadeModifier=true`, they are walled off from the cascade plumbing; they "cascade" only through the property engine's own propagation (`CvPropertyInfo.ChangePropagators`, scope-keyed Game↔Team↔Player↔City↔Unit↔Plot).

| Scope | Flat | Percent | Enabler (bool/set) | Indexed (keyed channel) |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Game/Team** | — | — | Project `EveryoneSpecialUnit`/`EveryoneSpecialBuilding` (valid-set); Vote `ForceCivics` (force-civic set) | Tech `TerrainTrades` (team terrain-trade set); Tech→Improvement/Route deposits; Civ `DisableTechs` |
| **Player** | Tech `FreeSpecialistCounts`, `DomainExtraMoves` | Trait family (12: SpecialBuilding/Building/Unit/SpecialUnit/UnitCombat production & happiness & research & XP mods); Religion `StateReligionCommerces` | Trait `CivicOptionNoUpkeep`; Civic `Hurrys`/`SpecialistValids`/`SpecialBuildingNotRequireds`; Trait `FreePromotionUnitCombats` | Trait `Specialist/ImprovementYieldChanges`, `Bonus/DomainFreeXP`; all Tech→Building/Specialist `buildingBoosts`; Bonus→Unit/Project production; Heritage `EraCommerceChanges` |
| **Area** | (FreeAreaBuilding grant is structural) | — | — | — |
| **City** | Bonus `Health`/`Happiness`; Corp `Health`/`Happiness`; Religion `HolyCityCommerces` | Corp `MilitaryProductionModifier`; Building inter-building prod/cost mods | Building `UnitCombatRetrainTypes`, `MayDamageAttackingUnitCombatTypes` | Bonus `YieldChanges`; all Bonus→Building `buildingBoosts`; Building UnitCombat-keyed (XP/strength/defense/prod/heal); Building `AidRateChanges`; Corp `Headquarter/Yields/CommercesProduced`/`CommerceChanges`/`YieldChanges`; Specialist `UnitCombatExperienceTypes`; Religion `GlobalReligionCommerces`; Improvement→Building yields |
| **Property engine (walled off)** | — | — | — | All `PropertyManipulators` (Building/Bonus/Feature/Terrain/Improvement/Unit/UnitCombat/Religion/Corp/Heritage/Promotion); `CvPropertyInfo.ChangePropagators`/`PropertyBuildings`/`PropertyPromotions` |

Two cascade-claim corrections worth surfacing:
- **`CvTechInfo.FirstFreeUnit`/`FirstFreeProphet`** are correctly `isCascadeModifier=false` — discrete event-time grants, not standing channels. Same logic correctly applied to `CvCivilizationInfo.FreeTechs`/`FreeBuildings`/`InitialCivics` (one-shot creation-time deposits, NOT cascade).
- **Trait `EraAdvanceFreeSpecialist`/`GreatPeopleUnitType`/`GoldenAgeOnBirth`** are correctly `isCascadeModifier=false` — single-FK effect *parameters*, not per-Type keyed channels. Good discrimination; don't let the "it's an effect" reflex promote these to channels.

---

## 4. Cycles & conflicts (same relationship described from both sides; ambiguous owners)

| # | The two sides | Canonical owner | Why |
|---|---|---|---|
| **C1** | `CvBuildingInfo.Bonus{Health,Happiness}Changes` (gated ON bonus) **vs** `CvBonusInfo.{Health,Happiness}` (intrinsic when present) | **CvBonusInfo** | The building maps are *conditional on bonus availability*; the bonus fields are the *same effect from the cause*. Inverting the building maps lands them next to the bonus's own fields — one home, the bonus. (Inventory note flags this mirror explicitly.) |
| **C2** | `CvImprovementInfo.RouteYieldChanges` (Improvement×Route) **vs** the route being the active yield-grantor | **CvRouteInfo** (chosen) — *but genuinely either* | Both are static templates; neither is player-state. Picked Route as "active grantor." This is a tiebreak, not a forced inversion. **Owner call (§7).** |
| **C3** | `CvBonusInfo.tradeProvidingImprovements`/`providedByImprovementTypes` (derived) **vs** `CvImprovementInfo.isImprovementBonusTrade` (forward) | **CvImprovementInfo** | The improvement declares it provides the bonus; bonus side is a derived reverse-index. Stays derived, no JSON. No conflict once the bonus side is recognized as recompute-on-load. |
| **C4** | `CvReligionInfo.shrineBuildings` (derived reverse) **vs** `CvBuildingInfo.HolyCity`/`ReligionType` (forward) | **CvBuildingInfo** | Forward FK on building is authoritative (registered via `addShrineBuilding` at building-load, CvBuildingInfo.cpp:1464); religion-side list is a derived index. No churn. |
| **C5** | `CvPromotionInfo.FreetoUnitCombats` (promo lists combat classes) **vs** the unit-combat reading "what free promos do I get" | **CvUnitCombatInfo** (reverse index) | Consumer asks per-combat-class (CvUnit.cpp:25971). Invert to a reverse index. Not scoped. |
| **C6** | `CvCivicInfo.CivicAttitudeChanges` (Civic×Civic, self-referential) | **CvCivicInfo** (stays, no inversion) | Pairwise, both sides are the same Info type — no *distinct* better owner. Correctly `inversionNeeded=false`; only re-shape (sparse keyed list) + diplomacy cascade. |
| **C7** | `CvCorporationInfo.CompetingCorporations` (Corp×Corp mutual-exclusion) | **CvCorporationInfo** (stays) — normalize symmetry on load | Self-referential constraint; "right side" trivially. The only work is making the relation symmetric at load (A competes B ⟺ B competes A). |
| **C8** | `CvProjectInfo.VictoryThresholds`/`VictoryMinThresholds` (Project×Victory) **vs** Victory defining its project requirements | **CvProjectInfo** (stays) — *arguably CvVictoryInfo* | Inventory keeps it project-side as intrinsic requirement data. Defensible but it's the one structural item where the *victory* could equally own it. **Low-stakes owner note (§7).** |
| **C9** | `CvCivilizationInfo.Leaders` (Civ lists leaders) **vs** Leader listing civs it may lead | **CvCivilizationInfo** (stays) | Pure membership; either home structurally valid, no effect to invert. Don't churn. |

No true *deadlock* cycles (A-effect-on-B and B-effect-on-A simultaneously) exist — all the bidirectional cases are forward-FK + derived-reverse (C3/C4) or same-type self-reference (C6/C7), which resolve cleanly.

---

## 5. Structural references that STAY (confirm, do not churn)

The 126 structural refs are overwhelmingly correctly homed. The pass must **not** touch these (reshape-to-JSON only, no re-home):

- **Prereq/gate FKs (#195 territory)** — every `Prereq*`, `requires*`, `forbids*`, `*MakesValid`, `ConstructCondition`/`TrainCondition` BoolExpr, `PrereqGameOption`, era/civ/civic-option/religion/corporation/bonus/building/tech/heritage prereqs across all Infos. These are dependencies, not effects. Stay on the dependent entity.
- **Lifecycle/identity FKs** — `Obsolete*`, `ExtendsBuilding`, `ObsoletesToBuilding`, `ProductionContinueBuilding`, `Replacement*`, `Improvement{Pillage,Upgrade}`, `Capture`, `UnitUpgrades`/`SupersedingUnits`, `OnUnitChangeTo` (Feature→Feature, despite the misnomer), `DerivativeCiv`, `Founds/PrereqCorporation`, `HolyCity`/`ReligionType`/`SpecialBuildingType`, `GreatPeopleUnitType`, `PropertySpawn{Unit,Property}`.
- **Intrinsic grant FKs (cause = the entity itself)** — `FreeBuilding`/`FreeAreaBuilding`, `FreeSpecialTech`, `ExtraFreeBonuses` (verified CvCity.cpp:8961/9136 — bonus is the grant *index*), `FreePromoTypes`, `FreeTraitTypes`, religion/corporation `FreeUnit`, `FirstFreeUnit`/`FirstFreeProphet`, civ `FreeTechs`/`FreeBuildings`/`InitialCivics`.
- **Own-stat keyed maps (situational, read about self)** — the *entire* CvUnitInfo combat/movement/invisibility surface (~50 fields: `Unit{Attack,Defense}Mods`, `Terrain/Feature{Attack,Defense}`, `*PassableTechs`, all `Visible*/Invisible*Changes`, work-rate mods, trap interactions), the parallel CvUnitCombatInfo surface, and the CvPromotionInfo combat/work/cloak surface. These are reshape-to-keyed-map only; **no inversion** — combat math iterates the bearer's own table, a Terrain/Feature/UnitCombat deposit would not be read more cheaply.
- **Categories / MapCategoryTypes** — membership tags on every Info. Always stay on the member.
- **Leader personality biases** — `ImprovementWeightModifiers`, `UnitAIWeightModifiers`, the 21 `*AttitudeThreshold` enums, `FavoriteCivic`/`FavoriteReligion`, `Traits`/`DefaultTraits`. Per-leader preference data — inverting `ImprovementWeightModifier` onto the improvement would be **wrong** (same improvement weighted differently per leader). Confirmed correct as-is.
- **Fixed-enum-keyed (no Info to invert TO)** — `PlotYieldChanges`, `RiverPlotYieldChanges`, all Yield/Commerce/Domain-indexed tables, `CvProcessInfo.ProductionToCommerceModifiers`, `CvProjectInfo.CommerceModifiers`, `CvFeatureInfo.YieldChanges`. Commerce/Yield/Domain/PlotType are bedrock enums, not Infos — these can't invert.

### Genuinely mis-homed structural ref flagged for note (not effect, but worth surfacing):
- **`CvCultureLevelInfo.SpeedThresholds`** (`inversionNeeded=false`, but the *per-GameSpeed scaling* is owner-wrong) — the threshold scalar stays on the culture level; the per-speed multiplier should move to a **GameSpeed-owned scale percent**. This is a static-scaling refactor (base scalar × `GameSpeedInfo.cultureThresholdScalePercent`), collapsing N parallel speed-indexed values. Not a cascade; flag it as a Wave-late cleanup so it isn't mistaken for "stays untouched."

### Dead/derived — DROP or keep derived, do not migrate:
- `CvBonusInfo.ImprovementChange` — **DEAD member**, NULL, never read. Drop entirely (soft removal; do NOT carry into JSON).
- `CvPromotionInfo.Disqualified/QualifiedUnitCombatTypes`, `CvUnitInfo.m_aiUnitUpgradeChain`, `CvReligionInfo.shrineBuildings`, `CvBonusInfo.tradeProvidingImprovements`, `CvTechInfo.m_leadsTo` — all derived/recompute-on-load. No JSON.

---

## 6. Wave plan (never ship half-and-half)

Each wave converts a destination entity **plus the cascade plumbing it needs**, end-to-end, so no consumer ever reads a half-migrated index. Ordering is driven by plumbing dependency: build the scoped-index machinery on the cheapest/most-isolated tier first, prove it, then fan out.

**Wave 0 — Cascade infrastructure (no data moves yet).**
Stand up the scoped-index machinery: Player-scope and City-scope per-Type effective-value indexes with deposit-on-event + recompute-on-load (all derived, **zero save-break** — they serialize nothing). Add `[CASCADE/*]`-tagged fidelity logging that asserts the new O(1) read equals the legacy sweep. This is the gate: nothing converts until parity logging is green. Reuse the derived-data-repository skeleton (TLazy versioning, bounded-staleness) — it already exists on the base objects.

**Wave 1 — Bonus channels (City scope, self-contained, mirror already correct).**
Convert `CvBonusInfo` first: its own `Health`/`Happiness`/`YieldChanges` already sit on the right side, so they validate the City-scope deposit path with no inversion risk. Then land the **14 Building/Unit/Project → Bonus inversions** next to them. Plumbing: City-scope per-building/per-unit/per-project bonus index, deposit on bonus-connect/-disconnect. `consumptionRelevantBonuses` is derived from exactly these maps — recompute it from the new home.

**Wave 2 — Tech channels (Player + Team scope).**
Convert `CvTechInfo`: the **11 Tech-conditioner inversions** (Building/Specialist `buildingBoosts`+`specialistBoosts` at Player scope; Improvement/Route at Team scope). Plumbing: deposit-on-tech-gain/-loss (mirrors the existing `processTech` loop at CvTeam.cpp:5713/6119/6068 — keep the deposit, drop the sweep). Bring `TerrainTrades`/`FreeSpecialistCounts`/`DomainExtraMoves` (already tech-owned, just reshape to channels) in the same wave. Also lands `Civilization.DisableTechs` (Player-scope disabled-set).

**Wave 3 — Trait channels (Player scope).**
Convert `CvTraitInfo`: all ~17 trait effect maps deposit into Player-scope per-Type indexes on trait-acquire. Collapse every struct-vector / int** into keyed maps. This wave is pure-Player-scope, no cross-tier, so it stresses the Player index hard before the harder mixed-scope waves. Resolve the `m_ppi` 2D wrapper blocker here.

**Wave 4 — Civic channels (Player/City scope, toggled).**
Convert `CvCivicInfo`'s 21 effect maps. These need **toggle-on-adopt / toggle-off-revoke** deposits (unlike Tech/Trait which are monotonic-ish). The `SpecialistValids`/`Hurrys`/`SpecialBuildingNotRequireds` enablers and `CivicAttitudeChanges` diplomacy cascade land here. Kill the `readPass3` `UnitProductionModifiers` dance.

**Wave 5 — Improvement / Terrain / Religion building-boosts (owner-decided).**
Only after §7 owner calls. Convert `CvImprovementInfo.buildingBoosts`, `CvTerrainInfo.buildingBoosts`, `CvReligionInfo.buildingBoosts`. Religion also lands its own `Global/HolyCity/StateReligionCommerces` as scoped channels (Player/City, gated on present/holy/state). `RouteYieldChanges` inverts to Route here (static, **not** plumbed through the cascade).

**Wave 6 — Inter-building deposits + Corporation/Heritage/Project/Vote channels.**
The 5 inter-building A→B deposit channels (Player/City). Corporation City-scope channels (HQ/produced/changes), Heritage `EraCommerceChanges` (Player, era-thresholded), Project `EveryoneSpecial*` (Game/Team valid-sets) and `CommerceModifiers` (Team), Vote `ForceCivics` (Game force-civic). These reuse machinery proven in Waves 1-5.

**Wave 7 — Reshape-only + cleanups (no cascade).**
Everything `inversionNeeded=false, isCascadeModifier=false`: the entire Unit/UnitCombat/Promotion own-stat surface (positional arrays → keyed maps), `FreetoUnitCombats` reverse-index, `CultureLevel.SpeedThresholds` GameSpeed-scaling refactor, `BonusProductionModifier`-on-Improvement de-densification, and the dead-member drops (`Bonus.ImprovementChange`). PropertyManipulators across all Infos migrate as self-reading sub-objects (untouched by cascade). No save-break in this wave (name-keyed format; derived state serializes nothing).

**Save-break posture across all waves:** every cascade index is **derived/recompute-on-load** → serializes nothing → no save-break. Type reorders are free (name-keyed). The only `@SAVEBREAK`-class risk is if any *currently-serialized accumulator* gets retired without its reader fixed in the same change — fix readers inline per the soft-removal rule.

---

## 7. Owner decisions

These need the owner before the relevant wave ships:

1. **Improvement/Terrain conditioner inversions (Wave 5 gate).** `ImprovementYieldChanges`, `GlobalImprovementYieldChanges`, `ImprovementFreeSpecialists`, `TerrainYieldChanges` are flagged `inversionNeeded=true` but the inventory itself hedges ("OR keep as building cascade keyed by Improvement/Terrain"). **Call needed:** invert onto `CvImprovementInfo`/`CvTerrainInfo` (full conditioner model) **or** keep building-side as a cascade channel keyed by the improvement/terrain? The Bonus/Tech inversions are unambiguous (player/team state); improvement/terrain "presence" is *plot-work* state, which is softer. Recommend: keep building-side channel keyed by improvement/terrain (the city already iterates worked plots) **unless** there's a cross-building aggregation win. Lower confidence than the Bonus/Tech calls.

2. **`ReligionChanges` → CvReligionInfo? (Wave 5).** Plan §57 explicitly leaves this as a question ("the religion?"). It's `religionSpread` granted by a building, gated on religion presence. **Call needed:** invert to religion, or treat as building-side religion-keyed channel? Recommend invert (matches the conditioner rule), but it's owner-sanctioned-pending by the plan's own wording.

3. **`UnitProductionModifiers` (Building→Unit) inversion (Wave 1 or defer).** Inventory says "closer to intrinsic… flag to owner." Unlike Bonus-production (clearly bonus-gated), this is just "building present → unit cheaper," which is barely-conditional. **Call needed:** invert to `CvUnitInfo.buildingBoosts`, or leave as building cascade keyed by Unit? Recommend leave building-side keyed by Unit (less churn, the conditioning is weak).

4. **`RouteYieldChanges` side (Wave 5 tiebreak — C2).** Both sides static; "either is defensible." Picked Route. **Call needed:** confirm Route, or keep on Improvement? Zero gameplay difference; pure single-source aesthetics. Confirm and move on.

5. **`Project.VictoryThresholds`/`VictoryMinThresholds` (C8, low stakes).** Kept project-side as intrinsic requirement data; could co-live on `CvVictoryInfo`. **Call needed:** leave on project (recommended — `VictoryPrereq` already pairs there) or consolidate victory-requirement data onto Victory? Not blocking any wave; decide at leisure.

6. **"This must stay" confirmations (no action, but owner should bless):** the entire **Leader personality bias** surface (`ImprovementWeightModifiers` especially — inverting onto the improvement is *wrong*), all **own-stat combat/movement/invisibility** maps (reshape-only), and **PropertyManipulators stays self-reading** (plan §163). These are explicitly *not* to be inverted; calling them out so the pass doesn't "fix" them.

7. **`CultureLevel.SpeedThresholds` GameSpeed refactor (Wave 7).** Not an inversion per the rulings, but the per-speed scaling genuinely belongs on GameSpeed. **Call needed:** do the base-scalar + `GameSpeed.cultureThresholdScalePercent` refactor now, or keep the explicit per-speed override list as an escape hatch? Recommend the derived scaling with override-only-when-present.
